preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 80% to win, leaving Austria just 2% on the underdog side. The 78-percentage-point gap reflects a heavily asymmetric contest in which the model sees little path to an Austrian victory.

The outcome distribution emphasizes Spain's dominance while keeping draws plausible. An 18% draw probability means the match carries material risk of a stalemate, but Austria's 2% win chance suggests the model expects Spain to control play and limit upset scope. The spread is wide enough to mark this as a clear favorite scenario rather than a closely matched knockout.

The sportsbook consensus assigns Austria nearly four times the model's win probability, at 8% versus 2%. This 6-percentage-point divergence is the main point of tension; both sources agree Spain is the strong favorite and a draw is realistic, but bookmakers see more Austrian upside than the statistical forecast does. Polymarket prices align more closely with the model across all three outcomes.
